data.title: Spain’s inflation rises to 3.1% in October
data.description:
• The inflation rate in Spain increased to 3.1% in October from 3% in September of 2025.
  • Core consumer prices in Spain increased 2.50% in October of 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWP), (NASDAQ:EUFN)

data.title: France inflation drops to 0.9% in October
data.description:
• The inflation rate in France decreased to 0.90% in October from 1.20% in September of 2025 and is lower than estimates of 1%.
  • On a monthly basis, the Consumer Price Index increased 0.10% in October over the previous month.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Siemens Energy AG GAAP EPS of €0.19, revenue of €10.4B; raises mid-term outlook
data.description:
• Siemens Energy AG press release (SMEGF): Q4 GAAP EPS of €0.19.
  • Revenue of €10.4B (+6.8% Y/Y).
  • Orders amounted to €14.2B, down 2.5% on a comparable basis (excluding currency translation and portfolio effects), primarily because of the high basis of comparison at Siemens Gamesa. Book-to-bill ratio (ratio of orders to revenue) was strong at 1.36. As a result, order backlog rose to a new high of €138B at fiscal year-end.
  • Free cash flow pre-tax largely followed the earnings trend and benefited from timing effects amounting to €1,327m (Q4 FY 2024: €932m).

  • Siemens Energy strives to reach a profit margin before special items in fiscal year 2028 in a range between 14% to 16% (before 10% to 12%). Siemens Energy intends to achieve a compound annual revenue growth on a comparable basis in the low-teens percentage range until fiscal year 2028 (before high single-digit to low double-digit).

  • Fiscal year 2025 results and fiscal year 2026 outlook are in line with expectations. Siemens Energy forecasts comparable revenue growth between 11% and 13% and a profit margin before special items between 9% and 11% in fiscal year 2026.

data.title: Merck seen nearing acquisition of Cidara Therapeutics in deal potentially above $3.3 billion
data.description:
• Merck (MRK) is nearing a deal to buy Cidara Therapeutics (CDTX), a drugmaker pioneering a long-acting antibody medicine that protects against flu, the Financial Times reported.
  • A deal valuing Cidara at a premium to its $3.3 billion market capitalization could be announced as soon as Friday, provided it did not hit any last-minute snags, the report said.
  • Merck was still vying with another pharmaceutical group for the biotech late on Thursday before the seller favoured its offer.
  • The exact price of the deal could not immediately be established. Any deal would likely include cash and the promise of future payments when clinical trial milestones are hit, the FT report said.
  • Cidara (CDTX) jumped more than 40% in post-market trading after the report.

data.title: Fronterra Energy to spin-off its Colombian infrastructure business
data.description:
• Frontera Energy (FEC:CA) announced a spin-off of its Colombian Infrastructure business, creating two independent companies: Frontera Exploration & Production and Frontera Infrastructure.

  • The proposed transaction is projected to be completed in H1 2026 and is subject to shareholder approval.

  • For the last twelve months ended September 30, 2025, Frontera Infrastructure had approximately $16.2M of operating EBITDA and $117.4M of infrastructure adjusted EBITDA and net debt to infrastructure distributable cash flow of 2.0x times.

data.title: Denarius Metals boosts fundraising target, launches concurrent private placement for total C$12.75M
DoorDash reveals October cybersecurity breach impacting its platform
data.description:
• DoorDash (DASH), in a post on its website, said that its team recently identified and shut down a cybersecurity incident that involved an unauthorized third-party gaining access to and taking certain user information.
  • There is no indication that data was misused for fraud, identity theft, the company said.
  • The food delivery platform deployed new enhancements to its security systems, implemented additional training and awareness, brought in an external firm to assist in the investigation, and referred the matter to law enforcement.
  • In 2019, a data breach at DoorDash exposed the information of roughly 5 million customers, Dashers, and merchants to an unauthorized party.
